title: centaurea drabifolia subsp. libanotica
intro: Centaurea drabifolia subsp. libanotica, also known as Mountain Centaury, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its perennial nature and distinctive appearance. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Mountain Centaury typically involve the use of its flowers, leaves, and roots, often in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures. While more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety, the plant has been employed in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health concerns.
